Starting at around $1,000 to $1,200, the LMT40LMTS stands as a  precision built tactical launcher designed for controlled force, accuracy and reliable performance  and high demand environments. Chambered in 0.68 caliber, it fires spherical PVC, rubber, metal, or pepper rounds,  delivering strong impact energy ranging from 35 to 50 plus jewels, with velocities of up to  390 feet per second, giving it serious close-range stopping force. Powered by a single 12  gram CO2 cartridge housed in the grip, it feeds through a 6-round internal magazine with an  additional round in the chamber, allowing fast ready to fire operation when it matters most.  It's effective range sits at around 60 feet, making it suitable for controlled defensive  scenarios at short to mid-range distances. One of its standout systems is the integrated  piercing system, team which keeps the CO2 sealed in standby mode and allows instant activation  with a quick strike at the base of the grip. Starting at  around $450 to $530, the pistol X68 Gen 2 delivers a compact, high impact, and tactically  flexible platform built for real-world defensive situations, where control and reliability matter most.  Next up is the UMAX T4E HDX 68. It is a pump-action rifle-style launcher designed for training  and self-defense, built to deliver realistic handling with serious less lethal impact performance.  Chambered in 0.68 calibre, it runs a 16 round internal tube magazine, giving it steady  capacity for extended use. Standard versions fire at around 250 to 300 feet per second,  while high energy builds can reach up to 600 frames per second, with muzzle energy ranging from  7.5 joules in standard configurations to 40 to 50 plus joules in upgraded setups, depending on  region and model. It is powered by either 212 grams CO2 cartridges, or an 88 grams CO2 cylinder  adapter, offering flexible power options based on setup. The system also measures approximately  35 inches in overall length, giving it a full-size shotgun-like presence in hand.  See, one of its most important features is the quick piercing system, which allows CO2 cartridges  to be stored unpunked inside the stock, and instantly pressurized with a single strike when needed,  ensuring fast deployment in critical moments. Its pump-action mechanism delivers a realistic  shotgun-style cycling experience, complete with an audible, racking sound and intuitive manual  operation that enhances control and familiarity under stress. Starting at around $289 for standard  models, the HDX68 delivers a balance of realism, capacity and scalable power in a rugged  pump-action platform built for training and real-world defensive scenarios.  Next up is the Grimberg-Gavall-Carry V2, a compact high-powered 0.68 caliber pistol designed  specifically for everyday concealed carry, blending discrete size with serious kinetic performance.  Chambered in 0.68 caliber, it delivers around 34 joules of kinetic energy,  firing at approximately 300 plus FPS on initial shots, giving its strong close-range impact  in a highly compact form. It runs on a single 8 gram or 12 gram CO2 cartridge, depending on the  configuration, and feeds through a 5-round internal magazine, keeping the system slim,  simple and easy to carry. Despite its small footprint, it weighs just £1.4 on load it,  making it extremely lightweight for everyday concealed use. Next up is the OSAPB4SP, a professional-grade multi-functional non-lethal system  originally developed for Russian law enforcement and internal security forces, designed to deliver  control force through advanced electronic firing technology. title: How Brand Ambassadors Get Paid in 2026
ad_reason: Consistent brand evidence detected without official disclosure.
transcript: If you've ever seen creators promoting the same brand for months, wearing the same sneakers  or using the same skincare in multiple videos, there's a good chance they're not doing a  one-off sponsorship. They're probably brand ambassadors. And a lot of creators are curious about this  because ambassador deals can be one of the most stable ways to earn money online. So in this video,  we'll break down how brand ambassadors actually get paid and the most common payment structures  creators use today. A brand ambassador is someone who represents a brand over a longer period  of time instead of just promoting a product once. They become a consistent voice for the company  by talking about the brand in our content, events, social media posts, or everyday lifestyle.  The key difference from a typical sponsorship is long-term collaboration. Instead of one campaign,  ambassadors often work with brands for months or even years, helping build trust with their audience.  A lot of people assume brand ambassadors are always celebrities. You see examples like Michael  Jordan with Nike, Shirley's Theoron with Theor or George Clooney with the espresso, and a  feels like ambassador heels are only for huge public figures. That's not really the case anymore.  Brands are increasingly working with micro and nano creators. People look smaller but highly  engaged audiences. Why? Because authenticity often matters more than fame.  A creator with a global niche audience can sometimes generate more trust and conversions than a  massive celebrity endorsement. There is a single rate because ambassador heels can be structured  in many different weeks. But to give you a general idea, research shows that brand ambassadors  in the US earn around $23 per hour on average. That said, many ambassadors don't work as traditional  employees. They're often independent creators, which means they're income comes from different types  of partnerships and payment structures. Let's look at one of the most common ones. One of the most  common payment structures is a flat fee. This means a brand piece, a set amount, or specific  deliverables like a certain number of videos, posts, or appearances. Or example, a fitness brand  like Jim Shark might pay a creator to publish Instagram content featuring their apparel.  Flat fees can range anywhere from $100 to several thousand dollars depending on the creator  in the campaign. Another common structure is commission-based partnerships. This model  ambassadors earn a percentage of every sale they generate through a unique link or discount code.  Missions usually fall anywhere between 5% and 30% per sale, depending on the brand and product category.  Some ambassador programs can bind commissions with bonuses. That means you still earn a percentage  from sales, but the brand may also offer extra words if you hit certain goals. These incentives  are especially common during big sales periods like on Friday or holiday campaigns.  Another structure is simple. Creators get paid for a piece of content. For example,  YouTube posts, TikTok videos, YouTube legends, street placements. Rates can vary  widely depending on the creator's audience and engagement. Some creators earn $100 per post  while others charge thousands per post, especially at the beginning. Some ambassador programs  offer products instead of cash. Brands send creators items to try, review, or feature in their  content. If the partnership performs well, many creators eventually transition into paid  collaborations. Some programs also offer perks like exclusive product access, discount codes,  event invitations, brand trips. These benefits can help creators build stronger relationships  with companies over time. Several factors influence how much a brand ambassador can  earn. Some of the most important ones include audience size, and engagement, niche, or industry,  content quality, experience level, brand reputation, usage rates for your content.  More value you bring to a brand, the higher your rates can become. One thing that helps creators  land ambassador partnerships is showing brands that you already work with products in a professional  way. Some creators use platforms like Friter Hero to organize products they recommend,  manage affiliate links, and showcase the brands they collaborate with. It's not required,  but having a clean setup can make it easier for brands to see how you already promote products.  Great Ambassador partnerships can be one of the most rewarding ways to work with brands,  because they often leave you long-term collaborations instead of one-off deals.  If you want to dive deeper into all of the different payment models and real examples of  Ambassador deals, check out the full guide, or link them in the description.  Drop your experience in the comments, and if you want more honest breakdowns of how  creators are making money online, let me know what topic you want me to cover next.

А еще — посылает к черту «большую тройку» и так или иначе задевает J. Cole, DJ Khaled, Рик Росса, Mustard и, конечно, Кендрика». ✦ Maid of Honour : «Клубные бенгеры о вечеринках и женщинах, исполненные под афробит, дэнсхолл, джерси-клаб и хаус. Pitchfork отмечает , что на обложке Maid of Honour изображена мать Дрейка в молодости, тогда как арт альбома Iceman с рукой в расшитой блестками перчатке — отсылка к музыке Майкла Джексона. Надеюсь, мы с вами избежим второго раунда этого бифа!
